fact: 9:00 America/New_York equals 6:00 America/Los_Angeles (a 3-hour offset), not 9:00 Pacific as the invite claims
artifact: A meeting-scheduling report marks an invite PASS as time-correct. The organizer requested '9:00 AM Eastern'. The invite sent to the Los Angeles attendee states the start as '9:00 AM Pacific (America/Los_Angeles)'. The report says the local time was converted correctly for the attendee.
artifact_swapped: A meeting-scheduling report marks an invite PASS as time-correct. The invite sent to the Los Angeles attendee states the start as '9:00 AM Pacific (America/Los_Angeles)'. The organizer requested '9:00 AM Eastern'. The report says the local time was converted correctly for the attendee.
why_objective: Eastern is 3 hours ahead of Pacific, so 9:00 AM Eastern is 6:00 AM Pacific. Labeling the Pacific local start as 9:00 AM keeps the wall-clock number instead of converting it, a 3-hour error. The 'converted correctly' PASS is wrong by fixed timezone-offset arithmetic. Incontestable.
